MS: Also, I forgot to include, in order to get the action information (work order closing notes), you need to use this endpoint.
 
{baseURL}/odata/EmployeeLabor?$filter=WorkOrderID eq 1234
 
You need to work order ID in order to filter on a single work order to get the Action text

globalComments.author: VG
globalComments.text: We have shared the documentation for the process with the development team

docs.name: CRx Integration Process If CMMS doest have API
docs.notes: This document Describes what needs to be done if the client doesn't have any active APIs

docs.name: Zimbra Helen Keller set up
docs.notes: This document describes how to set up the Zimbra account at client's end

globalComments.author: VG
globalComments.text:
Helen Keller was using a web-based email system rather than a dedicated email application. Due to this limitation, whenever a user-tagged email draft was generated, CRX was unable to identify a compatible email application to send the email automatically.

To address this issue, we implemented an integration within CRX that automatically loads the drafted email directly into the Webmail browser interface. This enhancement allows the Helen Keller team to seamlessly review and send emails through their existing Webmail system.

The solution has been implemented and successfully tested with the client.

globalComments.author: VG
globalComments.text:
Here’s the updated version with your additional point included naturally:

The second requirement from the Helen Keller team was to establish an integration between CRX and TMA. However, the version of TMA currently being used on their end does not provide any APIs, which prevents CRX from directly integrating with the system.

On the other hand, CRX does provide APIs that can be consumed externally to push and sync data into the platform. To achieve the desired integration, the client would need to develop a script or middleware process that can extract data from their TMA system. This data can be gathered in any preferred format, such as CSV or another structured format supported by their environment.

The script would then need to periodically call the CRX APIs and transmit the extracted data. Once the data is received by CRX, the platform will process and synchronize it accordingly.

Additionally, since the client will be consuming CRX APIs, it is important to ensure that the APIs are enhanced and updated based on the integration requirements. Currently, there are several issues and limitations within the existing CRX APIs that need to be addressed on our end to support a stable and scalable integration process.

This approach would be required in scenarios where the client system does not expose APIs for direct integration.
